title: `C extension swallows exceptions from PyObject_RichCompareBool in binding dispatch`
author: GrahamDumpleton
body:
## Summary

The C implementations of `FunctionWrapper.__call__`, `FunctionWrapper.__get__`, and `BoundFunctionWrapper.__call__` dispatch on `self._self_binding` using chains of `PyObject_RichCompareBool(...) == 1` comparisons. The `== 1` test conflates the error return `-1` with the false return `0`, so any exception raised during a comparison (e.g. from an `__eq__` on the binding object, or a transient `MemoryError` inside Unicode comparison) is silently swallowed. Subsequent comparisons in the same `||`-chain then overwrite the pending error indicator, and control falls through into a downstream `PyObject_CallFunctionObjArgs` that executes with a stale exception set — typically surfacing as a confusing `SystemError: ... returned a result with an exception set` instead of the original exception.

Eleven call sites across `src/wrapt/_wrappers.c` are affected. The pure-Python implementation propagates the exception correctly, so this is a C-extension-only divergence.

## Credits

Thanks to @devdanzin for the report via:

* https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/e1ac0c43dc4d4278ee82455da4859872
